---
title: "Projects"
description: "Selected computational physics, HPC, and scientific machine learning case studies."
---

Each case study follows the practical arc a technical reader usually wants: problem, approach, scale, validation, and artifact.

::: {.grid}
::: {.card}
### SCIMITAR3D solver development

Sharp-interface, multi-material reactive-flow framework. WENO + ghost-fluid integration cuts the grid-refinement overhead for equivalent accuracy by roughly 2.5&ndash;3&times;.

[Open project](scimitar3d.qmd)
:::

::: {.card}
### Production HPC campaigns

Microstructure-resolved mesoscale simulations on DoD HPC systems &mdash; 140M cells, 7,000 cores, ~2.8M CPU-hours per campaign &mdash; sized so hotspot statistics actually converge.

[Open project](hpc-dns-campaigns.qmd)
:::

::: {.card}
### HEDS framework

Diffusion / U-Net / CycleGAN microstructure generation paired with high-fidelity simulation to study damage&ndash;sensitivity behavior under shock loading.

[Open project](heds.qmd)
:::

::: {.card}
### Physics-aware ML benchmarks

DNS ground truth and physics-consistent evaluation for PARC and D-PARC neural surrogates of shocked energetic-material response.

[Open project](physics-aware-ml.qmd)
:::
:::

## Tools and stack

::: {.focus-strip}
<div>Fortran 90 / C++ / Python</div>
<div>MPI &middot; Slurm / PBS &middot; DoD HPC</div>
<div>PyTorch &middot; PARCtorch &middot; TensorFlow</div>
<div>ParaView &middot; Tecplot &middot; MATLAB</div>
:::

## See also

[Research](../research.qmd) for the scientific questions and methodological frame that shape these projects.
